title: cynoglossum trinervium
intro: Cynoglossum trinervium, also known as forked tongue, is a perennial plant belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas and the surrounding areas. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The health benefits of Cynoglossum trinervium include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
